rationale: The citing note explicitly says the 2023 paper "extends prior luxury-cue work centered on static visual design," and the cited 2016 paper is exactly a visual-design cue study showing that interstitial space increases perceived aesthetics/store prestige and downstream valuation/purchase. That makes this citation plausibly a theory/foundational comparison source for the luxury-cue literature rather than an obviously incidental mention, so it should be retained for full relation judgment.
